subject: Product Management
title: Opportunity Scoring
description: A micro-lesson that makes Opportunity Scoring usable.

hook.text: Tony Ulwick's opportunity scoring finds features users want most but are least satisfied with — the biggest gaps to fill.

buildup.text: Survey users on two dimensions: how important is this outcome, and how satisfied are you with current solutions?

discovery.text: The opportunity score formula: Importance + (Importance − Satisfaction).

twist.text: Features with high importance AND high satisfaction are table stakes — maintain them but don't invest heavily.

climax.text: Opportunity scoring grounds prioritization in user data instead of internal opinions — the user defines what's valuable.

punchline.text: Find what's important but unsatisfying. That's your highest-leverage opportunity.

quiz.question: What does opportunity scoring measure?
options: The gap between importance of an outcome and satisfaction with current solutions
The total number of feature requests
How much revenue each feature will generate
